titleStmt.title: SII 39.92: original edition by K. Karuppiah
No. 92. (A.R. No. 92 of 1916.) TIRUKKĀḶAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMNAD DISTRICT. KAKŌLANATHA TEMPLE-ON THE EAST WALL (INSIDE) OF THE SAME TEMPLE. TRIBHUVAṈACHAKRAVARTIN KŌṈĒRIṈMAIKOṆḌĀṈ-11TH YEAR.

summary.p: This inscription records that at the instance of a certain Maḷavarāyaṉ, the village of Muṉṉaikkuḍi in Puramalaināḍu which was purchased once by the temple from the Maravās of Vēlaṅguḍi in Pūṅguṉṟanāḍu was made tax-free by the king. Signed by Muḍivaḻaṅguvēḷāṉ Vikramapāṇḍya Aḻagiyapāṇḍya-viluppaṟaiyaṉ.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī tiripuvaṉaccakravatti koṉeriṉmai koṇṭā
ṭut tirukkoḷakkuṭic civadhammiśvamuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyil tevakaṉa
kkum civappirāmaṇaṟkuṭi Iṉṉāyaṉāṟkut tiruppaṭi māṟṟu Uḷḷiṭṭu veṇṭu ṉiva
ntaṅkaḷuk pūṅkuṉṟa nāṭṭu velaṅkuṭi maṟṟum [cayakkal Ittevaṟku ]
śvaranāmattāl vilai koṇṭaṉupavittu
yolaippaṭi nāṉkellaikkuḷpaṭṭa nikki
namun toṭṭamuṅ kuḷamuṅ kuḷapparappum
yokamum veli[ kūrala catram] poṉvariyum
ṉavum Uṭpaṭṭa Etirāmāṇṭu mutal Iṟaiyiliyāka Iṭappeṟa ve
ṇumeṉṟu Aiyyaṉ maḻavarāyaṉ namakkuc coṉṉamaiyil Iccivadharmmiśvāmu
ṭaiya nāyaṉāṟkut tiruppaṭimāṟṟuḷḷiṭ veṇṭu nivantaṅkaḷukku pūṅkuṉṟa nā
ṭṭu velaṅkuṭiyār pakkal Ittevar caṉṭeśvara nāmattāl vilaikoṇṭu
Aṉupavittu varukiṟa puṟamalaināṭṭu muṉṉaikkuṭi vilaiyolaippaṭi nāṉkel
laikkuḷppaṭṭa nirnilamuṅ karuñcey puṉceyu nattamun toṭṭamuṅ kuḷamuṅ kuḷa
pparippum Uṭpaṭa nilaṅkaṭamaiyantarāyam viṉiyokamu velikkāla m po
ṉvari ciṟṟāyamum maṟṟum Eppeṟpaṭṭaṉavum Uṭpaṭa Ivvāṇṭu mutal
tevatāṉa Iṟaiyiliyāka Iṭṭu variyilā Eḻuttiṭṭa Uḷvari naṅ keḻvi
yum taraccoṉṉom kaikkoṇṭu Ippa
cantirātittavay celvatāka kalliluñ cempilu veṭṭivittu kuṭuta
ka Ivvūrum kūṟṟattuppatup vāṉa Āṇṭapirāṉ
ṉāṉa Aḻakiya pāṇṭiya viḻupparaiyaṉ Eḻuttu yāṇṭu 10 1 vatu nāṉ cā
Ivai Aṇṭa perumaṇanallūr Amaiyaṉ
nāṉa vikkirama Uttaramantiri Eḻuttu Ivai
palla vārāyaṉ Eḻuttu
Digital edition of SII 39.92 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
